objective: Deliver a local-only, single-file MarkItDown document-ingestion flow that converts approved document types into canonical Knowledge with provenance, idempotent duplicate handling, fail-closed error behavior, and quarantined-file deletion.
invariants: No Concept exists without complete provenance (file identity, content digest, ToolVersion identity, conversion time, Execution/Trace).
OKF Markdown with provenance and Git history is canonical; FTS5, embeddings, sqlite-vec, KnowledgeChunk, and IndexState are derived.
A Concept is not durably ingested until its valid OKF change has a successful Git commit.
All persistent canonical and derived writes cross the single Core KnowledgeWriter boundary.
Converted content is always untrusted data and cannot authorize effects or alter Policy.
One accepted file produces one Concept in this flow.
Exact duplicates are idempotent and never create a second Concept version.
The MarkItDown ToolVersion is pinned, read-only, local-only, network=none, and plugins disabled.
The original quarantined file is deleted after persistence succeeds and on Concept deletion.
No batch upload, archive/ZIP ingestion, URL ingestion, cloud OCR, plugins, or external network access exists in this slice.
decisions: The product owner explicitly selected issue #15 for local-only MarkItDown document ingestion.
Issue #15 is owned by the Tool Lab bounded context for delivery; Knowledge owns canonical persistence, supersession, and deletion.
Issue #15 is the first tracer bullet under a new SPEC-P0-document-ingestion spec, separate from SPEC-P0-domain-discovery.
Minimum accepted file types for the first slice: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, CSV, TXT, Markdown.
The first version is local-only; no cloud, no URL ingestion, no plugins, no ZIP/archive, no external network.
A target KnowledgeBundle is not required before upload.
Single-file upload only; no batch upload in v1.
Upload performs conversion and canonical persistence in one command.
The original quarantined file is deleted after persistence and on Concept deletion.
Exact duplicates are idempotent and return the existing Concept.
Meaningly changed re-ingestion creates a new Concept and records supersession.
Encrypted or corrupted files fail closed.
Scanned documents that require OCR are surfaced as degraded.
When retries are exhausted, no alternate converter is introduced first; then provenance-bearing user input if available; otherwise suspend execution.
The repo has no src/ directory or monorepo skeleton yet (Phase 0 WP-0.4 incomplete); the first slice begins with Node.js contract tests and module skeletons, not a real runtime.
GitHub Project fields configured: Status Todo, Phase 0, Bounded Context Tool Lab, Risk R1, Spec SPEC-P0-document-ingestion, Human Review Required, Size M.
The spec worktree was bootstrapped at .worktrees/spec-document-ingestion with spec/document-ingestion and feature/15-markitdown-local-ingestion.
implementation_state: Task 1 in progress. The issue #15 event-storming record and this HandoffBrief have been drafted on the main checkout. No source code has been written yet. The spec worktree .worktrees/spec-document-ingestion is active on feature/15-markitdown-local-ingestion. The implementation plan at my-docs/plan/2026-07-14-markitdown-document-ingestion-implementation.md is committed on main.
open_loops: The written event-storming record awaits product-owner acceptance before issue #15 can move to Review.
The repo lacks a src/ directory and monorepo skeleton; the first code slice uses Node.js contract tests and module skeletons.
next_action: Commit the issue #15 OKF artifacts on main, then start Task 2 (pin the MarkItDown tool boundary) in the spec worktree with a failing contract test.
